register.note: hero.png opens to a bare title masthead with a tiny placard floating in a wide upper-right void and no specimen at all, where ciechanowski-watch/00-viewport.png leads its landing straight into a large rendered watch portrait; forced 0 and earned.
there is no hero to depict the mechanism (the arrow-vs-contour move only appears mid-scroll in the See/Run canvas, never promoted to a poster like seeing-theory's residual squares); forced 0.

the See canvas carries a mono title and a live grad/slope/height readout strip, but the blue arrow itself is untagged and the right-angle-to-contour claim lives in adjacent prose, short of distill-momentum/02 where each small-multiple is individually titled with its math consequence captioned in-figure.

the masthead chrome is warm and Distill-soft, but the focal contour landscape is visibly aliased (native crop shows chunky stair-stepped band edges) unlike the clean anti-aliased strokes in ciechanowski-watch/02-scroll-40pct.png, and the empty hero void breaks first-paint focal hierarchy.

statics show explanatory, steppable apparatus — draggable point with a swinging/stretching gradient arrow, ascent/descent toggle, a 01/05 beat stepper, and a released greedy-climb in Break — transitional not decorative, at parity with the behavior-per-panel intent of 3b1b-gradient-descent/02 (judged conservatively, no unseen polish rewarded).

the lab grammar holds — teal=selected/live, blue=the gradient/model arrow matching the blue grad readout, amber/slate as a legitimate elevation ramp (no all-red error dilution), and prose terms pair the darkened viz-ink hue with underline — as consistently held as the green/blue class split in r2d3-trees/00-viewport.png.

hero.note: needs a wide ambient specimen portrait (per DESIGN.md / LinearRegressionHero) of the contour landscape with the gradient arrow standing on it, smoothly rendered with one explanatory load-motion, so the page has a poster-worthy face instead of an empty masthead void.

assessment.note: the experiment is genuinely playable (drag the point) and every Check option gets process feedback, but the 06 TRANSFER item is a three-row MCQ with no interaction or open prompt.

blocking: Add a wide specimen hero before the masthead — an ambient contour-landscape portrait with the gradient arrow on it, one explanatory load-motion, no readouts — so the exhibit has a poster-worthy protagonist (currently absent; forces hero dims to 0).
Re-render the contour landscape anti-aliased (it is visibly stair-stepped/pixel-blocky at native resolution) so the page's focal object reaches the Distill/Ciechanowski smoothness bar.

Tag the arrow in-figure (label it grad-f and mark the right angle where it crosses a contour) so the canvas self-documents instead of leaning on adjacent prose, per distill-momentum's per-panel captions.
Make the Transfer item interactive or open-ended (e.g. drag onto a multi-basin landscape and predict the basin) rather than a third MCQ.
verdict.summary: Strong colour grammar and steppable motion, but no specimen hero and an aliased centerpiece keep two dimensions at 0 and two at 2 — below the register-3 bar.
